yields.note: L. rhamnosus GG pykDECTD grew similarly to WT in glucose (no defect)

growth_records.cite: She, Fukang et al. Allosteric regulation of pyruvate kinase enables efficient and robust gluconeogenesis by preventing metabolic conflicts and carbon overflow. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01131-24. [PMC11834443]
growth_records.snippet: The resulting L. rhamnosus GG pykDectd mutant grew similarly to wild-type cells in glucose medium but displayed a growth defect in pyruvate medium ... diluted into fresh mMRS supplemented with either 100 mM glucose, 100 mM of malate, or 100
growth_records.notes: Cross-species validation in the lactic acid bacterium L. rhamnosus GG (ATCC 53103). Glycolytic (glucose) condition: WT and pykDECTD grow comparably. Growth measured by OD600 plate reader; numeric growth rate figure-only (Fig. 2i / Fig. S7). mMRS composition only partially itemized in text (MgSO4 amount not given); medium_composition_stated=true but partial. No secretion quantified. pH not stated.

yields.note: pykDECTD shows growth defect on pyruvate vs WT

growth_records.cite: She, Fukang et al. Allosteric regulation of pyruvate kinase enables efficient and robust gluconeogenesis by preventing metabolic conflicts and carbon overflow. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01131-24. [PMC11834443]
growth_records.snippet: The resulting L. rhamnosus GG pykDectd mutant grew similarly to wild-type cells in glucose medium but displayed a growth defect in pyruvate medium (Fig. 2i; Fig. S7), confirming that the pyruvate kinase [ECTD role is conserved].
growth_records.notes: Gluconeogenic pyruvate condition for L. rhamnosus GG WT. WT grows on 100 mM pyruvate; the constitutively-active pyruvate-kinase mutant is defective. Growth rate measured (OD600) but numeric value figure-only. No secretion/uptake numbers in text. pH not stated; mMRS partial composition.

growth_records.cite: Chao-Lei Zhang et al. Dynamic immobilization of bacterial cells on biofilm in a polyester nonwoven chemostat. Bioresources and Bioprocessing (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40643-024-00732-0. [PMC10992621]
growth_records.snippet: The chemostat held 1.0 L worth of culture in total. The dilution rate was fixed at about 0.045 h−1. By automatically adding sodium hydroxide, the fermenter's pH was kept at 6.0 and the temperature was maintained at 37℃ ... To keep the chemo
growth_records.notes: Continuous chemostat at fixed D = 0.045 h-1; at steady state mu = D = 0.045 h-1 (growth rate = dilution rate). Doubling time td = ln2/0.045 = 0.6931/0.045 = 15.4 h. Steady-state free-cell OD 3.96 ± 0.06 (no nonwoven). Product (lactic acid) not quantified as a rate. L. rhamnosus is a prokaryote (Gram-positive lactic acid bacterium).
